body:
meta-expression issue: https://github.com/link-assistant/meta-expression/issues/7

Context:
meta-expression currently needs the same user-facing behavior that calculator already has: generate a GitHub issue URL with environment, input, result, steps, and Links Notation-style diagnostic payload. For issue #7 we reused the calculator pattern in meta-expression, but the code still has to live locally.

Proposal:
- Extract the calculator report issue helper into a small reusable API or package surface.
- Keep the API independent from React/i18n so consumers can provide labels and page state.
- Include successful and failed result shapes, alternative interpretations, and reproduction steps.
- Keep tests that assert title/body/labels are present in the generated GitHub issue URL.

Suggested consumer shape:
```ts
generateIssueUrl({
  repository: "link-assistant/meta-expression",
  input,
  result,
  linksNotation,
  steps,
  environment,
});
```

This would let meta-expression, calculator, and other link-assistant browser tools share the same reporting behavior instead of copying report builders.

comments.body:
We should also export other useful features of the calculator as API of the library.

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

title: Expose reusable issue-report URL builder for meta-expression integration
